titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 28

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=110 status=live modified=2021-04-01 18:51:37.544143+00 | translation moa=111 status=live modified=2016-06-07 11:03:03.493815+00

div.p:
Omnibus hoc scriptum uisuris uel audituris Alexander de striuelin . Salutem eternam in domino. Noueritis quod
cum venerabilis in Christo pater Dauid dei gratia Episcopus Sancti Andree de consensu prioris ⁊ conuentus eiusdem
loci propter diuini cultus augmentum . ⁊ ad instanciam meam capelle mee de Laurencistun
cantariam concescisset  ne matrix Ecclesia de Eglisgirge ex tali concessione aliquam paciatur
iacturam  promisi fideliter quod ego et heredes mei faciemus dicte capelle honeste exspensis propriis
deseruiri ⁊ omnes obuenciones siue oblaciones ad dictam capellam prouenientes faciemus in
tegre ad dictam matricem Ecclesiam de Eglisgirge deferri . eam in omnibus indempnem con
seruantes . capellanus eciam quicunque dicte capelle deseruiet  priusquam ibi celebret sacramentum
fidelitatis dicte matrici Ecclesie prestabit. Et ad recognicionem subiectionis ego et heredes mei sin
gulis annis in perpetuum . In nundinis de munros vnam libram cere persoluemus. Quod si ego uel
heredes mei uel capellanus qui in dicta capella ministrauerit contra aliquod predictorum venerimus  promitto firmiter
pro me ⁊ heredibus meis quod usque ad condignam satisfaccionem a celebracionem diuinorum in predicta capella
cessabitur . In cuius rei testimonium vna cum sigillo Archidiaconi sancti Andree presenti scripto sigillum meum
apposui Datum apud Ormestun Nonas decembris . Anno gracie . m . cc . xliii .

To all who shall view or hear this deed, Alexander of Stirling wishes eternal salvation in the Lord: May you all know that since the venerable father in Christ, David by the grace of God bishop of St Andrews, by the consent of the prior and religious community of the same place and for the augmentation of divine worship, and at my urging, granted to my chapel at Lauriston a chantry: lest the mother church of Egglisgrig should suffer any loss from such a grant, I promised faithfully that I and my heirs would cause its own expenses properly to be paid to the said chapel, and we would cause all obventions or offerings coming in to the said chapel to be transferred entirely to the said mother church of Egglisgrig, keeping it without injury or loss in all things, the chaplain also, whoever it be, that shall minister at the said chapel, before he celebrate the sacrament there shall take an oath of fealty to the said mother church, and as acknowledgement of submission, I and my heirs shall pay out one pound of wax each year on the Fair of Montrose in perpetuity. But if I or my heirs, or the chaplain who ministers in the said chapel, should act against any of the aforesaid things, I firmly promise for myself and my heirs that until appropriate satisfaction [has been made] there shall be no celebration of divine service in the aforesaid chapel. In testimony whereof together with the seal of the Archdeacon of St Andrews I have applied my seal to the present deed. Given at Ormiston on the Nones of December [5 December] in the year of grace 1243.
